title: Codereview pass 1: MIC covers LLSec byte but signature excludes it - intentional divergence
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:57-75 vs 200-232

**Observation:** _compute_mic_data() includes LLSec byte in MIC coverage (line 71), but _build_signable_data() explicitly excludes LLSec from signature (documented at lines 219-221).

**Analysis:** This is intentional and correct:
- Signature authenticates the sender (covers immutable security-relevant data)
- MIC protects integrity (covers wire format including flags)

The signature doesn't need LLSec because it authenticates via the pubkey. The MIC needs it to detect flag corruption.

**Worth defending:** Don't 'unify' these two functions - they serve different security purposes.
close_reason: Design documentation - MIC/signature coverage divergence is intentional
